ops.body: Closed in cookiesync v0.10.4 (origin/main 064494d, cask published). C1 (measured probe timings on a locked Mac): on locked yasyf-home `ioreg -n Root -d1 -a` = 17ms, `netstat -anv -p tcp` = 4ms — both trivial; the filed premise that netstat was the slow syscall was REFUTED. Active-screen-share netstat number deferred (yasyf-home had only an idle :5900 listener, no ESTABLISHED session); implausible to matter (netstat is a kernel socket-table dump, not slowed by WindowServer screen-sharing). C2: auth_status now runs an ioreg-only probeKeybag; the screen-share netstat probe — which auth_status never read (keybagLocked ignores ScreenShared) — is off the doctor hot path. whoami/request_consent/peerIsLive keep the full ProbeSession for consent routing. The real masking cause was the cache-heal mutex (3c889d6), fixed alongside.
